description: v1.0.1 baseline performance, measured with the PACKAGED executable (dist/LecturePack/LecturePack.exe --run-acceptance) at tag v1.0.1-real-media-verified (c3ff16c), before any v1.1 changes. Same hardware as final comparison. Sequential pipeline: Inspect -> Extract Audio -> Transcribe -> Detect Slides -> Align (+ Export on demand).

hardware.gpu: AMD Radeon RX Vega 56 8GB (driver 31.0.21923.11000, Vulkan 1.3.301 instance / 1.3.260 device)

engine.backend: whisper.cpp CPU (bundled whisper-cli.exe, ggml CPU dlls)

egypt_full_lecture_reference_v1.0.1.source: docs/evidence/v1.0.1/full_lecture_run.md (packaged run recorded during v1.0.1 validation)

ui_responsiveness_notes: Context Repair dialog generates proposals synchronously in __init__ on the GUI thread; with an LLM provider configured every chunk is a blocking urllib call (60s timeout) -- the window freezes and any provider exception at construction can take down the dialog (user-reported crash).
Slide detection runs in a QThread but the review list loads full-resolution PNGs as QIcons on the GUI thread.
Detection decode path uses cv2.VideoCapture random seeks at native resolution for every 1fps sample plus 0.25s stability probes and +1s persistence probes.

bottleneck_analysis.detect_slides_dominant: Detection costs 98.5s for a 363s excerpt (0.27x realtime) due to full-resolution random seeks; transcribe and detect run sequentially, so total is their sum.
expected_gains: Sequential downscaled FFmpeg-piped analysis decode instead of per-sample native-resolution seeks
Run transcription and slide detection concurrently after audio extraction (aim: slower branch + overhead instead of sum)
Optional whisper.cpp Vulkan backend on the RX Vega 56

ollama_baseline_bench.note: Installed models benchmarked with a schema-constrained /api/chat repair chunk (6 segments, temperature 0, JSON schema format).

qwen3.5:9b.corrections_quality: correct fixes but leaked '[12]' id prefix into corrected_text
